text.title: heuristic-evaluation Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for heuristic-evaluation as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Evaluators collaborating or discussing before individual evaluations are complete
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Early discussion anchors all evaluators to the first person's findings. Later evaluators fail to explore areas already covered and underweight areas not mentioned. Independent evaluation is the core quality mechanism of the method.

failure-mode.description: Vague findings: "The UI is confusing" or "Navigation is hard to use"
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Cannot be reproduced, prioritized, or actioned. Minimum valid finding: location + heuristic + specific problem + severity + recommendation. All five fields are required.

failure-mode.description: Single evaluator evaluation presented as comprehensive
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: One evaluator finds ~35% of issues. Claiming comprehensive coverage from a single-evaluator review sets false expectations. Always note evaluator count in the report methodology section.

failure-mode.description: Agent declaring "no violations found" for a heuristic without explicit reasoning
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Either the interface is genuinely excellent for that heuristic, or the evaluator did not look thoroughly. A "no violations" declaration requires a brief explanation of why the heuristic is satisfied, not just absence of findings.

failure-mode.description: An agent evaluating the same interface multiple times with the same prompt tends to reproduce the same findings
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Vary the user scenario and focus heuristic to increase coverage

failure-mode.description: Agents inflate severity ratings for visually prominent issues and underrate violations in less conspicuous locations
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening

failure-mode.description: Heuristic #8 (aesthetic and minimalist design) is the most subjective; agent ratings for "excessive visual noise" depend heavily on training examples
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Be explicit about design philosophy in prompts; validate ratings with domain experts

failure-mode.description: Agents cannot evaluate heuristic #7 (flexibility and efficiency of use) for keyboard shortcuts and power-user features without interacting with the product
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Supplement agent evaluation with interactive testing for keyboard-dependent workflows

failure-mode.description: Agent declaring "no violations found" for a heuristic without explanation
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Either the interface is genuinely excellent or the agent did not look hard enough; always ask for explicit reasoning per heuristic
